title: synostemon kakadu
intro: Synostemon kakadu, also known as the Kakadu plum or Billygoat plum, is a plant species native to the Northern Territory of Australia. The plant is a rich source of vitamin C, containing up to 3,300 milligrams per 100 grams, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. The medicinal properties of the Synostemon kakadu plant include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat a range of health issues, from skin conditions to gastrointestinal problems. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's fruit is often used to make a tea or juice, while the leaves can be used to make a tincture or infused in hot water for medicinal purposes.
